steps.title: Compare hardening types
steps.content: The researchers found that bulkheads have a stronger negative correlation with waterbird community integrity than riprap does. Since a higher IWCI score means higher integrity, the best illustration must compare a bulkhead-heavy site with a riprap-heavy site and show the bulkhead-heavy site having a lower score. It must preserve both hardening type and the direction of the IWCI difference rather than merely comparing total shoreline alteration.

steps.title: Reject incomplete comparisons
steps.content: A compares high hardening with low hardening without isolating type. B compares two sites where bulkheads dominate, so it lacks a riprap contrast. D compares equal and different percentages, which does not test bulkheads against riprap. The remaining comparison directly shows lower integrity at the bulkhead-heavy site. The answer is C.
